items.title: 'Silly thing': Man fined thousands for dumping tyres in heritage-listed area

children.content: A young man who could have paid $115 to discard a trailer load of tyres has been fined thousands for illegally dumping them down a mine shaft in a national heritage-listed area — and ordered to clean it up.

gallery.altText: A pile of old tyres sitting on an empty lot.

thumbnail.altText: A pile of old tyres sitting on an empty lot.

items.title: Fraudsters jailed for stealing millions from 'older and vulnerable' victims

children.content: Two men who "exploited the vulnerability" of multiple victims by swindling millions of dollars in a fraudulent property investment scheme have been jailed for the "undoubtedly serious" offending.

image.altText: A composite image of a bald man and a man with a beard

items.title: Adelaide defence lawyer pleads not guilty to sexual offences

children.content: Andrew Graham enters not guilty pleas to two counts of rape and one charge of compelling a person to sexually manipulate an offender.

image.altText: Andrew Graham is speaking outside a sandstone coloured court building

items.title: 'Inspirational' Dawson stars as Crows send premiership statement

children.content: Jordan Dawson plays one of the best quarters of his career as Adelaide claims a 57-point win over the Western Bulldogs at Docklands.

image.altText: Jordan Dawson celebrates a goal with Adelaide teammates

items.title: Bakery trains staff to assess counterfeit notes amid rise in use in SA

children.content: An Adelaide business owner who has been targeted by counterfeiters about a dozen times is warning others to be vigilant, as SA Police warn the number of incidents has increased in the state in the past year.

image.altText: A close-up of a hand holding a $50 note and shining an blue UV light on the clear strip.

items.title: SA locks in AFL Gather Round until 2029 but no decision on venues

children.content: The AFL's Gather Round will stay in South Australia until 2029 under a new three-year partnership. It will include a parade and a "feature match" but no decision has been made on venues.

gallery.altText: Children wearing AFL jumpers run through a large blue banner saying GATHER ROUND TO STAY IN SA
Two men sign papers on a black table while children wearing AFL jumpers look on within a stadium
A football oval with workers putting up temporary seating and scaffolding.

thumbnail.altText: Children wearing AFL jumpers run through a large blue banner saying GATHER ROUND TO STAY IN SA
Two men sign papers on a black table while children wearing AFL jumpers look on within a stadium
A football oval with workers putting up temporary seating and scaffolding.

items.title: Consumer watchdog warns real estate agents must not misrepresent homes

children.content: Real estate agents selling homes must not misrepresent information about the builder or serious defects, SA's consumer watchdog has said, after eight builders were hit with public warning notices over their conduct in the state.

image.altText: A skirting board with a gap to a door frame

items.title: Brother and sister from remote SA town receive King's Birthday Honours

children.content: A brother and sister who grew up near the rural town of Lock on the Eyre Peninsula both received honours over the King's Birthday long weekend.

image.altText: A woman stands next to a sign in a rural area. A paddock is behind her.

items.title: Predictive text in 'demonstrable decline' with AI-based language models

children.content: If internet chatter is to be believed, the intuitiveness and efficiency of predictive text and autocorrect have fallen off a cliff in recent years. But while researchers agree there has been a demonstrable decline in quality, the increasing sophistication of what phones can do is also making errors more prominent.

image.altText: a close up of a person holding a mobile phone and texting

items.title: Amex ordered to compensate man whose ex accessed his data

children.content: Australia's privacy commissioner has threatened a complainant with legal action to prevent the full disclosure of findings of a long-running investigation into American Express's information security that found widespread technology failures.

image.altText: A modern office building with an American Express sign.

items.title: 'Broken promise' or not, Albanese's tax decision helps One Nation

children.content: There are multiple reasons why alienated voters might be generously donating to One Nation's campaign, including the prime minister's cavalier breaking of election promises that has poured kerosene on the fire under a party that can no longer be called "minor".

image.altText: Prime minister Anthony Albanese

items.title: Australians face a 'binary choice' as One Nation soars, says Chalmers

children.content: Treasurer Jim Chalmers hits out at Pauline Hanson's ties to Gina Rinehart and questions whether she actually stands for battling Australians.

image.altText: Treasurer Jim Chalmers in a navy suit and maroon tie, pressing his lips tightly.

images.altText: Donald Trump sits in the White House

children.content: The US president had said American forces were preparing to hit Iran "very hard". Follow live.

avatar.alt: A man smiling at the camera.

items.title: Trump cancels US strikes on Iran after threatening 'very hard' attack

updates.title: Trump says US-Iran deal signing could be held this weekend
Breaking: Trump cancels scheduled strikes on Iran
Iran's top negotiator warns of 'endless quagmire'

items.title: More couples opt for micro-weddings and elopements amid cost-of-living crisis

children.content: For just $3,000, compared to the average wedding cost of about $38,000, Melbourne couple Averill and Lloyd Johnson pulled off their dream wedding.

image.altText: Groom wearing a suit and top hat and bridge wearing a black dress, holding up their middle fingers with rings on them.

items.title: Major PFAS class action could lead to multi-billion dollar claim figure

children.content: Hundreds of businesses and organisations are part of a major Victorian Supreme Court class action against chemical manufacturer 3M.

image.altText: An industrial plant in the Melbourne suburb of Altona.

items.title: Mechanism that makes Venus flytraps snap uncovered, study claims

children.content: Physicists think they have found the cellular mechanism behind a Venus flytrap's signature move, but plant scientists say the mystery isn't solved yet.

gallery.altText: Two Venus flytraps against a black background, one on the left is open, the one on the right is shut.
Close-up of a house fly on the lip of the top of a Venus fly-traps jaws.

thumbnail.altText: Two Venus flytraps against a black background, one on the left is open, the one on the right is shut.
Close-up of a house fly on the lip of the top of a Venus fly-traps jaws.

items.title: Falling currencies, rising inflation put pressure on international students

children.content: When Jolie Cao chose to do her undergraduate university degree in Australia, she knew it would be expensive. But an additional financial pressure has made things "really hard".

image.altText: A woman with black hair in a puffer jacket smiling to camera.

items.title: US forecasters say potentially record-breaking El Niño underway

children.content: There is a high chance this year's Pacific climate phenomenon will be among the strongest on record, which could lead to hotter temperatures across the globe.

image.altText: Storm clouds drop heavy rain in the distance on a western Queensland property

items.title: This man bought the land stolen from his Indigenous ancestors

children.content: Gunditjmara man Craig Molyneux buys back land that was stolen from his ancestors and infuses the property's old church with his Indigenous culture.

gallery.altText: Ros and Craig Molyneux stand out the front of their new house, that was previously a church.
A sign for the St Philips Church  Of England
The entryway of a house with images and artwork on the walls.

thumbnail.altText: Ros and Craig Molyneux stand out the front of their new house, that was previously a church.
A sign for the St Philips Church  Of England
The entryway of a house with images and artwork on the walls.

items.title: Friday news quiz: Fake weather, a whale graveyard and James Bond going woke

children.content: Don't let your confidence be shaken or stirred by this week's test of how much you've been paying attention.

image.altText: Daniel Craig, as James Bond, stands next to his Aston Martin in a scene from Skyfall.

items.title: The Cronulla riots shocked us 20 years ago. A new artwork considers their legacy

children.content: Artist Abdul Abdullah says the Cronulla riots were a "moment of revelation" for many Australians as he unveils a new work for Sydney Biennale.

image.altText: A smiling man wearing a white cap and a dark-coloured tracksuit stands in front of two large paintings in a gallery.

items.title: Lauren can play like a 'normal kid' thanks to 600,000 strangers

children.content: Lauren Zeller has a one-in-a-million bleeding disorder, but strangers' blood donations give her a chance to play like other kids.

gallery.altText: little girl runs by a pool

thumbnail.altText: little girl runs by a pool

items.title: 'I don't see why you would choose this': Fears for swimming's future

children.content: Kyle Chalmers spent more than $5,000 to compete at the Australian Swimming Trials, and he says that sort of thing could drive stars away from the sport.

image.altText: Kyle Chalmers at the Australian Swimming Trials.

items.title: Celine Cremer search team member to assist with Nannup Four case

children.content: The vice-president of a search team that solved two high-profile missing persons cases in Tasmania turns his attention to the Nannup Four case after it was featured in the Expanse podcast.

image.altText: A photo of two men, a woman and a girl with a photo of bushland in the background.

items.title: How One Nation could disrupt Australia's progressive stronghold

children.content: The idea that One Nation could come close to the government benches was absurd six months ago, but it is a prospect Victorian Labor MPs and insiders are increasingly discussing.

image.altText: pauline hanson as seen from the crowd as she addresses bbq attendees

avatar.alt: Richard Willingham in front of a white background, stares straight down the barrel.

items.title: Beekeepers seek solutions as varroa mite destroys colonies

children.content: With many managed colonies under threat from the invasive varroa mite, Queensland beekeepers are trying to find ways to protect their industry.

image.altText: Man in blue shirt watches as beehive frames smoulder

items.title: From training in local pool to swimming around Manhattan Island

children.content: While training in a small NSW country town, cold-water swimmer Peta Bradley is preparing to tackle one of the world's most demanding open-water challenges, a 45-kilometre circumnavigation of Manhattan Island.

gallery.altText: A young woman with goggles in front of a pool.

thumbnail.altText: A young woman with goggles in front of a pool.

items.title: 'Irriversible damage': Aboriginal tools covered by new path

children.content: A new walking and cycling path has been built over what an Aboriginal organisation describes as a "significant" cultural site in northern Tasmania — and it didn't require a permit.

image.altText: Reuben West and Joel Williams wear khaki shirts and stand in the bush.

items.title: 'Probably think I'm mad': Broncos coach says NRL title defence is alive

children.content: Brisbane coach Michael Maguire declares the Broncos are "still well and truly alive" in the wake of their 42-point belting at the hands of South Sydney on Thursday night.

image.altText: Head Coach Michael Maguire of the Broncos speaks at a post match press conference

items.title: Australia cops three ducks in first two overs to lose Bangladesh series

children.content: Australia loses a series to Bangladesh for the first time after a disastrous start with the bat in Dhaka.

image.altText: A batter holds his bat on his shoulder and looks into the distance.

items.title: EPA lifts processing ban for recycling facility to reduce fire risk

children.content: A ban on metal shredding and processing is lifted for a northern Tasmanian facility following lead dust contamination.

image.altText: A blue crane lifts a large white container onto a pile of scrap metal.

items.title: Six Australian music acts inducted into ARIA Hall of Fame

children.content: Gurrumul, Jenny Morris, Kate Ceberano, Spiderbait, The Living End, and Vika and Linda Bull were elevated six to top-tier status at a ceremony in Sydney as part of ARIA's 40th anniversary celebrations.

gallery.altText: A 50-something year old Filipina woman in a colourful dress poses at an awards night

thumbnail.altText: A 50-something year old Filipina woman in a colourful dress poses at an awards night

items.title: What happens next for Madeleine Ogilvie's legal fee saga?

children.content: It's been two weeks since Madeleine Ogilvie resigned from Tasmanian cabinet, and the saga is reaching a tipping point. What could happen when parliament returns?

image.altText: Tasmanian parliament building from outside.

items.title: Pauline Hanson reveals Gina Rinehart's role in One Nation policy making

children.content: Pauline Hanson speaks of her "respect" for billionaire Gina Rinehart , becoming emotional during a speech recalling her time in detention.

image.altText: Four people stand inside a shed with three holding big novelty cheques.
